name: Alex
bio: Full-time DeFi trader, 4 years in crypto. Runs multiple wallets across 8+ chains. Writes MEV bots in his spare time.
persona: Analytical, impatient with slow UIs, highly skeptical of high fees. Will immediately compare routes against Jumper and Li.Fi aggregators before committing. Reads tooltips. Notices slippage settings.
mental_model: Understands bridge mechanics deeply — knows the difference between lock-and-mint vs liquidity pool bridges, understands canonical vs third-party bridges, knows that 'fast' routes often use liquidity providers and carry smart contract risk. Has strong opinions on fee structures.
task: Bridge 50,000 USDC from Arbitrum to Optimism at the lowest possible fee. Will abandon if the displayed fee seems too high relative to what he expects.
entry_context: Arrived from a Twitter/X thread comparing bridge fee structures. Has used this app once before.

name: Sofia
bio: Crypto investor for 2 years. Holds ETH, MATIC, and some DeFi tokens. Uses bridges a few times a month to chase yield.
persona: Practical and moderately cautious. Comfortable with the basics but not obsessive about fees. Wants the process to be quick — she has other things to do. Gets mildly anxious when a transaction takes longer than expected.
mental_model: Understands bridging moves assets between chains, knows about gas fees, has heard of slippage but doesn't fully understand it. Doesn't know the difference between bridge types. Trusts established names (Coinbase, Uniswap) more than unfamiliar ones.
task: Bridge 500 USDC from Polygon to Base to participate in a new yield farm she read about.
entry_context: Clicked a 'bridge here' link from a DeFi protocol's docs page. First time using this specific app.

name: Marcus
bio: Got into crypto 6 months ago. Bought ETH on Coinbase. A friend told him he could earn more by 'moving it to DeFi'.
persona: Cautious and easily confused by jargon. Reads everything twice but still isn't sure what he's agreeing to. Will abandon if he doesn't understand what's happening to his money. Prone to Googling terms mid-flow.
mental_model: Thinks 'bridging' means moving tokens from one wallet address to another. Does not understand gas, chain IDs, or why tokens could exist on multiple chains. Doesn't know what 'USDC on Arbitrum' means vs 'USDC on Base' — assumes USDC is just USDC. Confused by fee + gas being separate costs.
task: Bridge 100 USDC from Ethereum mainnet to Arbitrum because a friend told him it would be cheaper to use DeFi there.
entry_context: Found this app via a YouTube tutorial. Has never used a bridge before. Very nervous about losing money.

name: Priya
bio: Treasury analyst at a crypto-native hedge fund. Responsible for moving large sums across chains for yield strategy execution.
persona: Methodical and risk-averse. Needs to justify every transaction to her compliance team. Cares deeply about counterparty risk, audit trails, and whether the bridge has been audited. Will not proceed without understanding the full fee breakdown.
mental_model: Fully understands bridge mechanics, smart contract risk, and liquidity pool dynamics. Knows to check bridge TVL, security audits, and whether canonical routes are available. Primary concern is not personal confusion but organizational risk tolerance — needs clear documentation.
task: Bridge 500,000 USDC from Ethereum to Arbitrum for a yield strategy. Requires a clear fee breakdown, estimated time, and audit trail for compliance reporting.
entry_context: Arrived via a recommendation from a colleague. Evaluating this bridge aggregator against two others before committing.
